title: HAT PASS: multi-witness verification across five primitive Pythagorean directions
_fixture_purpose: General witness fixture. Verifies HAT₁=e/d=C/(G+F), HAT₂=(d-e)/(d+e)=F/(G+C), spread s=E/G=HAT₁²/(1+HAT₁²) for five primitive directions. Confirms the Price-QA bridge holds across the Pythagorean tree, not just the root.

general_theorem.statement: For all d>e>0 with gcd(d,e)=1 and d+e odd: HAT₁=e/d=C/(G+F), HAT₂=(d-e)/(d+e)=F/(G+C), s=E/G=HAT₁²/(1+HAT₁²)
general_theorem.price_connection: H. Lee Price 2008: HAT₁ and HAT₂ are the 'half-angle tangents' used to construct Fibonacci boxes. Each column of the Fibonacci box [[e,d-e],[d,d+e]] is a (numerator,denominator) pair of one HAT fraction. The three children of a node in Price's Pythagorean tree are generated by three operations on this box. These operations are exactly QA generation matrices M1, M2, M3 of the Barning-Hall tree.
general_theorem.proportionality_note: HAT₁ must be read as C/(G+F) from the triple, not as an independently reduced fraction. The numerator C and denominator G+F carry QA element meaning: C=green quadrance, G+F=hyp+odd_leg=2d². This proportional form is what Price encodes in the Fibonacci box columns.

validation_checks.description: at least 3 witnesses with all HAT checks passing
